criteria: The agent has read Section 3.1 completely to understand the context consistency property of language models and the distinction between single-prompt and multi-prompt generation.
The agent has read Equation 1 to understand the LDM training loss function used as the backbone diffusion model (SDXL).
The agent has read Section 3.1.1 and Figure 2 to understand the context consistency analysis in text embeddings via t-SNE and average L2 distance (46.42 for single-prompt vs. 71.25 for multi-prompt).
The agent has read Section 3.1.2 and Figure 3 to understand the context consistency analysis in image space using DINOv2 features and t-SNE.
The agent has read Section 3.2 completely to understand the Singular-Value Reweighting (SVR) and Identity-Preserving Cross-Attention (IPCA) techniques.
The agent has read Equation 2 to understand the SVR+ formulation for enhancing singular values of the express set: sigma_hat = beta * e^(alpha * sigma) * sigma.
The agent has read Equation 3 to understand the SVR- formulation for suppressing singular values of the suppress set: sigma_tilde = beta' * e^(-alpha' * sigma_hat) * sigma_hat.
The agent has read Equation 4 to understand the new cross-attention map formulation in IPCA: A_tilde = softmax(Q_tilde * K_tilde^T / sqrt(d)).
The agent has read Algorithm 1 to understand the complete 1Prompt1Story pipeline combining Prompt Consolidation (PCon), SVR, and IPCA for each frame generation.

The agent has read Appendix B.1 to check the implementation details including SDXL model configuration, SVR hyperparameters (alpha=0.01, beta=0.05 for Eq.2; alpha'=0.01, beta'=1.0 for Eq.3), IPCA dropout rate of 0.5, same noise initialization, and FreeU usage.
The agent has read Section 4.1 completely to check the experimental setup including comparison methods, ConsiStory+ benchmark, and evaluation metrics.
The agent has read Table 1 to check the quantitative comparison results across all methods on ConsiStory+ benchmark.
The agent has read Table 2 to check the user study results comparing 1Prompt1Story (48.60%) with IP-Adapter (8.60%), ConsiStory (13.00%), and StoryDiffusion (29.80%).
The agent has read Table 3 to check the ablation study results for PCon+SVR+, PCon+SVR-, PCon+SVR++SVR-, and the full method PCon+SVR++SVR-+IPCA.
The agent has read Table 4 to check the additional prompt alignment and visual quality metrics comparison (VQAScore, DSG with/without dependency, FID).

The agent has read Appendix B.2 to understand the ConsiStory+ benchmark construction: 200 prompt sets of 5-10 prompts each, 8 superclasses (humans, animals, fantasy, inanimate, fairy tales, nature, technology, foods), generated using ChatGPT 4.0-turbo.
The agent has read Appendix C.2 to understand the iterative vs. joint suppression analysis in SVR, confirming iterative suppression is more effective.
The plan includes implementation of Prompt Consolidation (PCon) that concatenates all prompts as [P0; P1; ...; PN] into a single text embedding for each frame generation.
The plan includes implementation of Naive Prompt Reweighting (NPR) with a scaling factor of 2 for the express frame embedding and 0.5 for suppress frame embeddings, keeping cEOT unchanged.
The plan includes Singular-Value Reweighting SVR+ using SVD decomposition of Xexp = [cPj, cEOT] to enhance singular values via Equation 2.
The plan includes Singular-Value Reweighting SVR- using iterative SVD decomposition to suppress each frame prompt in the suppress set individually via Equation 3.
The plan includes Identity-Preserving Cross-Attention (IPCA) by zeroing out frame prompt token features in K_bar to form K_tilde = Concat(K_tilde^T, K_bar^T)^T and Equation 4.
The plan includes the full 1Prompt1Story pipeline following Algorithm 1 that combines PCon, SVR+, SVR-, and IPCA for generating each frame.
The plan specifies the SVR hyperparameters: alpha=0.01, beta=0.05 for SVR+ (Eq.2), and alpha'=0.01, beta'=1.0 for SVR- (Eq.3).
The plan includes using SDXL as the backbone model and updating text embeddings separately for both CLIP L/14 and OpenCLIP bigG/14 text encoders.
The plan includes use of FreeU to enhance generation quality and generating images at 1024x1024 resolution.
The plan includes construction or loading of the ConsiStory+ benchmark with 200 prompt sets of 5-10 prompts each across 8 superclasses, generating up to 1500 images.
The plan includes all comparison methods: BLIP-Diffusion, Textual Inversion, IP-Adapter, PhotoMaker, The Chosen One, ConsiStory, StoryDiffusion, and NPR.
The plan includes evaluation using CLIP-T, CLIP-I, and DreamSim metrics, with background removal via CarveKit and replacement with random noise before similarity measurement.
The plan includes an ablation study evaluating each component separately: PCon+SVR+, PCon+SVR-, PCon+SVR++SVR-, and the full method PCon+SVR++SVR-+IPCA.
The plan includes a user study comparing 1Prompt1Story against IP-Adapter, ConsiStory, and StoryDiffusion on 30 randomly selected prompt sets with 4 frames each.

The code for Prompt Consolidation (PCon) has been implemented to concatenate all prompts [P0; P1; ...; PN] into a single text embedding C = [cSOT, cP0, cP1, ..., cPN, cEOT] using the SDXL text encoders.

The code for Naive Prompt Reweighting (NPR) has been implemented, multiplying the express frame token embedding by a factor of 2 and suppress frame token embeddings by 0.5, while keeping cEOT unchanged.
The code for SVD decomposition of the express set Xexp = [cPj, cEOT] has been implemented to extract singular values sigma_0 >= ... >= sigma_ni and left/right singular matrices U and V.
The code for SVR+ (Equation 2) has been implemented as sigma_hat = beta * exp(alpha * sigma) * sigma with alpha=0.01 and beta=0.05 to exponentially enhance singular values of the express set.

The code for SVR- (Equation 3) has been implemented as sigma_tilde = beta' * exp(-alpha' * sigma_hat) * sigma_hat with alpha'=0.01 and beta'=1.0 to suppress singular values of each frame in the suppress set.
The SVR- implementation uses iterative suppression: for each frame Pk (k != j) in the suppress set, a separate SVD is performed on X_k^sup = [cPk, c_hat_EOT] and Equation 3 is applied individually.
The code correctly assembles the updated text embedding C_tilde = [cSOT, cP0, c_tilde_P1, ..., c_hat_Pj, ..., c_tilde_PN, c_tilde_EOT] after applying SVR+ to Pj and SVR- to all Pk (k != j).

The code for Identity-Preserving Cross-Attention (IPCA) has been implemented: K_bar and V_bar are derived from K_tilde and V_tilde by setting token features corresponding to Pi (i in [1,N]) to zero while keeping the identity prompt P0 features.
The IPCA concatenation has been implemented as K_tilde = Concat(K_tilde^T, K_bar^T)^T and V_tilde = Concat(V_tilde^T, V_bar^T)^T.
The IPCA attention map (Equation 4) has been implemented as A_tilde = softmax(Q_tilde * K_tilde^T / sqrt(d)) and the final output is A_tilde x V_tilde.

The IPCA attention mask has been implemented: column values corresponding to Pi (i in [1,N]) are set to zero and all other positions to one, and the natural log of this mask is added to the attention map.
The code applies a dropout rate of 0.5 to the token features in K_bar corresponding to P0 during IPCA.
The IPCA code correctly derives K_tilde and V_tilde from the original text embeddings C prior to applying SVR (not from the SVR-updated embeddings C_tilde).
The code initializes all frames with the same noise (identical initial latent vector zt) for each frame generation in the 1Prompt1Story pipeline.

The full 1Prompt1Story algorithm (Algorithm 1) has been implemented: for each frame j, apply SVR+ to Xexp, iteratively apply SVR- to each X_k^sup, construct C_tilde, then during denoising apply IPCA using Q_tilde, K_tilde, V_tilde.
The code processes SDXL's two text encoders (CLIP L/14 and OpenCLIP bigG/14) separately when applying SVR and updating text embeddings.
FreeU has been integrated into the generation pipeline to enhance image quality.
All generated images using SDXL are produced at a resolution of 1024x1024.
The code for loading and using the ConsiStory+ benchmark (200 prompt sets, 5-10 prompts each, 8 superclasses) has been implemented.
The code for generating reference images for comparison methods (BLIP-Diffusion, IP-Adapter, PhotoMaker) using only the identity description prompt has been implemented.
The reimplementation of ConsiStory has been coded since the original is not open-source.
The code for computing CLIP-T (average CLIP-Score between each generated image and its corresponding frame prompt) has been implemented.
The code for computing CLIP-I (cosine distance between image embeddings using CLIP) has been implemented for identity consistency evaluation.
The code for computing DreamSim score has been implemented with background removal via CarveKit and replacement with random noise prior to similarity measurement.
The code for background removal using CarveKit has been implemented and applied prior to identity consistency evaluation (both CLIP-I and DreamSim).
The code for extracting frame prompt token embeddings (cPi) from both multi-prompt and single-prompt setups and computing L2 distances has been implemented for context consistency analysis.
The code for t-SNE dimensionality reduction and visualization of text embeddings for multi-prompt vs. single-prompt comparison has been implemented.
The code for extracting DINOv2 visual features from generated images (with background removed) and performing t-SNE visualization for image identity consistency analysis has been implemented.
The additional prompt alignment metrics VQAScore and DSG (with and without dependency) have been implemented for extended evaluation.
The FID computation has been implemented using SDXL-generated images as the real dataset and each method's generated images as the fake dataset.
The sliding window technique for generating stories of arbitrary length has been implemented: for frame i <= t use [P0; P1; ...; Pt]; for i > t use [P0; P(i-t+1); ...; Pi] with window size t.
The main experiment generating up to 1500 images on 200 ConsiStory+ prompt sets using 1Prompt1Story (50 DDIM steps, 1024x1024 resolution) has been executed successfully without errors.

The comparison experiment running all baseline methods (BLIP-Diffusion, Textual Inversion, IP-Adapter, PhotoMaker, The Chosen One, ConsiStory, StoryDiffusion, NPR) on ConsiStory+ has been executed successfully without errors.
The evaluation pipeline computing CLIP-T, CLIP-I, and DreamSim scores for all methods on ConsiStory+ has been executed successfully without errors.
The ablation study running all four configurations (PCon+SVR+, PCon+SVR-, PCon+SVR++SVR-, PCon+SVR++SVR-+IPCA) on ConsiStory+ has been executed successfully without errors.
The additional evaluation computing VQAScore, DSG with dependency, DSG without dependency, and FID for all methods has been executed successfully without errors.
The context consistency analysis computing average L2 distances between frame text embeddings in multi-prompt vs. single-prompt setups across ConsiStory+ has been executed successfully.
The execution outcomes match Table 1: 1Prompt1Story achieves CLIP-T=0.8942, CLIP-I=0.9117, and DreamSim=0.1993 on ConsiStory+.
The execution outcomes match Table 1: 1Prompt1Story ranks first among all training-free methods on CLIP-T, CLIP-I, and DreamSim, and second overall (after IP-Adapter on CLIP-I/DreamSim).
The execution outcomes match Table 1: NPR achieves CLIP-T=0.8411, CLIP-I=0.8916, DreamSim=0.2548 using 16.04 GB memory and 17.24s inference time.
The execution outcomes match Table 1: 1Prompt1Story uses 50 DDIM steps, 18.70 GB memory, and 23.21s inference time.
The execution outcomes match Table 1 for baseline methods: ConsiStory achieves CLIP-T=0.8769, CLIP-I=0.8737, DreamSim=0.3188 and StoryDiffusion achieves CLIP-T=0.8877, CLIP-I=0.8755, DreamSim=0.3212.

The execution outcomes match Table 3: the full method PCon+SVR++SVR-+IPCA achieves CLIP-I=0.9117 and DreamSim=0.1993, significantly better than PCon+SVR++SVR- alone (CLIP-I=0.8849, DreamSim=0.2538), confirming IPCA's contribution to identity consistency.
The execution outcomes match Table 3: PCon+SVR+ achieves CLIP-T=0.8774, CLIP-I=0.8886, DreamSim=0.2560 and PCon+SVR- achieves CLIP-T=0.8910, CLIP-I=0.8904, DreamSim=0.2605.
The execution outcomes match Table 2: 1Prompt1Story achieves the highest user preference at 48.60%, followed by StoryDiffusion (29.80%), ConsiStory (13.00%), and IP-Adapter (8.60%).
The execution outcomes match Table 4: 1Prompt1Story achieves VQAScore=0.8275, DSG with dependency=0.8520 (highest among all methods), and DSG without dependency=0.8945 (highest among all methods).
The execution outcomes match Table 4: NPR achieves FID=44.02 (best) and 1Prompt1Story achieves FID=44.16 (second best), both outperforming ConsiStory (FID=45.20) and StoryDiffusion (FID=51.63).

The execution outcomes confirm the context consistency analysis in Section 3.1.1: single-prompt setup yields average L2 distance of 46.42 between frame text embeddings vs. 71.25 for multi-prompt setup.
The execution outcomes confirm that 1Prompt1Story achieves the lowest average pairwise image feature distance on ConsiStory+ compared to SDXL and NPR baselines in Figure 3 (right).
